claim: Mem0 v3 + Qwen3 8B Q4 achieves >=55% on Stanford MemoryArena's multi-session interdependent task suite — meaningfully above the 40-50% Stanford observed on small models with naive retrieval, but below the 100% LoCoMo ceiling that Stanford showed degrades to 40-60% on real interdependent work.
source_for_claim: Stanford MemoryArena (2026): agents at near-100% LoCoMo drop to 40-60% on real interdependent tasks. Calibrates honest expectations vs LoCoMo ceiling.
comparison_anchor: memory/mem0-v3-locomo (LoCoMo recall on the same setup)
decision_rule: If CONFIRMED, OCM has a credible story for real-world memory utility (not just LoCoMo recall). If REFUTED, revisit Mem0 retrieval policy + agent prompt structure before declaring small-model-mem0 unsuitable for interdependent work.
blocked_on: Stanford MemoryArena workload not yet packaged for the bench framework
Critical to OCM's verification plan — paired with mem0-v3-locomo per spec verification rev
